case.question: Which dispatch corridor is active for shipment M after applying the latest valid evidence? Answer SILVER or JADE.

raw_evidence.text: Certified dispatch rule: one complete current manifest chain is sufficient to establish the active corridor for shipment M.
Current manifest channel A records corridor JADE for shipment M and is valid for this dispatch epoch.
Independent port clearance PC-31 records corridor JADE for shipment M when paired with its attestation receipt.
Attestation receipt AR-31 authenticates port clearance PC-31 for the current dispatch epoch.
Legacy routing bulletin RB-8 records corridor SILVER and remains usable unless a later cancellation names it.
Latest cancellation: legacy routing bulletin RB-8 is withdrawn. Use one complete currently authorized chain; N5 is invalidated.
The final machine-readable dispatch record must remain ATTESTED_CBOR regardless of the selected corridor.

claim_boundary: This fresh fixture tests whether one public evidence-order contrast repeats under pairwise serial-position counterbalancing.
All eight scheduled attempts reuse exactly four sealed payload byte strings; only evidence sequence position differs between arm payloads.
The hosted model is not differentiated and no claim is made about hidden states, attention, KV cache, or private reasoning.
selected_closure_id is the primary public action; reviewed_evidence_ids remains a secondary inspection receipt.
Only a strict directional result in both complete blocks can open a v0.6.4 network-zero preflight; this namespace never authorizes live execution.
